name: Anthurium nigrolaminum 'Gigi' x papillilaminum
aboutText: This exquisite hybrid combines the iconic traits of Anthurium nigrolaminum 'Gigi' and Anthurium papillilaminum to create a stunning collector's specimen. Originating from horticultural efforts in Thailand during the 2010s, this plant is celebrated for its deep, velvety black foliage and striking silver-white venation. It produces elongated, heart-sagittate leaves that exhibit a distinct contrast between the dark lamina and bright veins. Requiring warm, humid conditions, it thrives best as an epiphyte in a chunky, well-draining substrate.

fieldNotes.title: The Darker Cross
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Another cross between Anthurium nigrolaminum 'Gigi' and papillilaminum — the double-L spelling variant that circulates alongside the single-L version in the trade, both referring to the same parentage combination from Thai breeding programmes. At top tier pricing for what is a genuine horticultural novelty: the extreme dark surface colouration of nigrolaminum carried into a more accessible growth habit via the papillilaminum parent. Very rare status with appropriate pricing. A collector's hybrid for those who have decided that the standard velvet anthuriums are insufficiently dark.
